fields.problem_statement: The Instructor College (TIC) has pivoted from being a training provider to being a group that acquires, operates, and supports driving school businesses, but the current public website still leads with nationally recognised training course language ('Complete TLI41222', 'we train the instructors'). This creates two compounding problems: first, a compliance risk where ASQA states only registered RTOs can advertise or deliver VET, and TIC's current site implies it delivers nationally recognised training which may not be legally accurate; second, a positioning mismatch where the site fails to reflect TIC's actual business model as an investor, operator, acquirer, and services platform for driving schools, undermining acquisition conversations and industry credibility with potential sellers, partners, and recruits.

users.goal: Evaluate whether TIC is a credible, trustworthy acquirer and understand the sale process, continuity assurances, and next steps for a confidential enquiry
users.context: Business owners who have built driving schools and are exploring exit or succession options. They need reassurance about staff continuity, brand preservation, and a clear simple path to begin a conversation.

users.role: Driving school operator needing support services
users.goal: Understand what operational support services TIC offers, see clear outcomes and practical details, and initiate a service enquiry
users.context: Operators of existing driving schools who may not be selling but need help with compliance, fleet management, marketing, bookings, or other operational challenges.

users.role: General credibility audience (partners, recruits, media, prospects)
users.goal: Quickly understand who TIC is, what it does, its scale and track record, and how to get in contact
users.context: Includes potential business partners, job candidates, journalists, and general industry observers who need a clear picture of TIC's identity and positioning as a driving industry group.

scope_in: Reposition TIC as group/operator/acquirer brand (core messaging pivot)
Home page: hero, what TIC is, proof strip, value creation model (Acquire/Operate/Support), group brands preview, services preview, acquisition CTA, insights preview, contact CTA
About page: who we are, story, leadership, operating model, values, FAQ
Our Group page: overview, brand directory rendered from markdown content model, regions/locations, individual brand detail pages
Acquisitions page: why owners choose TIC, what we look for, what happens after sale, process steps, seller FAQ, confidential enquiry form
Contact page: general enquiry, acquisition enquiry, service enquiry, media/partnerships forms
Utility pages: privacy policy, terms, cookie policy, accessibility statement, sitemap, 404 page, thank-you confirmation page
11ty (Eleventy) static site generator with collections, pagination, global data, Image plugin
Cloudflare Pages deployment (Workers functions only where needed)
Nunjucks or Liquid templating
Vanilla JS or Alpine.js for client-side interaction
GSAP only if tightly controlled motion is needed
Cloudflare Turnstile for form spam protection
Cloudflare Web Analytics
WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility target
Core Web Vitals compliance (LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, INP < 200ms)
Structured data: Organisation and breadcrumb JSON-LD
Responsive mobile-first design
Dark/light layered sections, strong typography, editorial layouts
Documentary-style photography direction, minimal stock imagery
Content model: brands/*.md, services/*.md, insights/*.md, case-studies/*.md with defined frontmatter schemas
Remove or noindex any pages that imply TIC delivers nationally recognised training (ASQA compliance)
scope_out: Interactive tools: portfolio map, acquisition fit self-check, service maturity quiz (Phase 3)
SPA architecture or heavy frontend frameworks
Any content implying TIC delivers nationally recognised training
constraints: ASQA compliance: must remove or noindex any pages implying TIC delivers nationally recognised training; only registered RTOs can advertise/offer/deliver VET
Templating must be Nunjucks or Liquid
Client-side JS limited to vanilla JS or Alpine.js; no heavy SPA frameworks
GSAP permitted only for tightly controlled motion; no heavy animation libraries
Performance constraints: fast LCP, minimal CLS, lightweight interaction especially on mobile
Design direction: dark/light layered sections, strong typography, editorial layouts, documentary-style photography
Optional infrastructure: Cloudflare R2 for media storage, Workers for server-side functions
Phased delivery: Phase 1 (core pages) must ship independently of Phase 2 (services, insights, case studies) and Phase 3 (interactive tools)

success_criteria.criterion: Site launches on pages.dev with all Phase 1 pages live and responding
success_criteria.measurement: Successful Cloudflare Pages deployment with all defined routes returning HTTP 200

success_criteria.criterion: Zero compliance risk — no page implies TIC delivers VET or nationally recognised training
success_criteria.measurement: Content audit of every page passes with zero instances of VET delivery language, RTO advertising, or nationally recognised training claims

success_criteria.criterion: Core Web Vitals pass on all pages
success_criteria.measurement: Lighthouse/PageSpeed audit: LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, INP < 200ms on all Phase 1 pages
axe-core automated audit returns zero critical violations on all Phase 1 pages
Form submits successfully, Cloudflare Turnstile validates, confirmation page displays

success_criteria.criterion: Brand directory renders all brands from markdown content model
success_criteria.measurement: All brands/*.md entries render on the Our Group page with correct title, region, summary, and links

success_criteria.criterion: Structured data validates for Organisation and breadcrumb
success_criteria.measurement: Organisation and breadcrumb JSON-LD pass Google Rich Results Test with zero errors

prior_art: Tiny (tiny.com) — plain-English acquisition pitch, proof metrics, founder-friendly tone
Halma (halma.com) — purpose + portfolio + operating model site structure
Permanent Equity — acquisition criteria, seller reassurance, operational transparency
Breakthrough Energy — three-part model analogous to Acquire/Operate/Support
Inspired Education / Nord Anglia — group site positioned above operating brands
Total Drive / MyDriveTime — driving school industry service page references
Lightship, Joby, Shift5 — premium motion and content architecture (Awwwards winners)
TIC's existing website (to be replaced) — current site implies RTO training delivery, creating the compliance and positioning problems this project solves

positioning.core: A driving industry group that acquires, operates, and supports driving school businesses.
positioning.brand_promise: We help driving schools grow through better systems, stronger operations, and specialist industry support.

phased_rollout.phase_1: Home, About, Our Group, Acquisitions, Contact + utility pages
phased_rollout.phase_3: Interactive tools, advanced filters, validated service lines

content_model.brands: brands/*.md — title, region, service_areas, brand_summary, website_url, featured_image, status, order
content_model.services: services/*.md — title, audience, outcomes, features, CTA
content_model.insights: insights/*.md — title, category, summary, author, published_date, related
content_model.case_studies: case-studies/*.md — title, business_type, challenge, intervention, outcomes, metrics, testimonial
